timeline_html:
Summary. Gina’s shedding has been present for over a year and tends to come in waves. She’s recently begun noticing new baby hairs, showing that her follicles are waking back up. 🌱

Your hair-loss triggers.

  • Major illness with fever (resolved; Mar–Mar 2020)
  • Pregnancy, birth, or miscarriage (resolved; Dec 2011–Aug 2012)
  • Menopause or perimenopause symptoms (ongoing; started Feb 2025)

Timeline forecast. The short illness in 2020 likely caused a temporary shed that resolved long ago, while hormonal shifts from perimenopause have been a more gradual, ongoing influence since last year. Based on our lag model and your recent baby hairs, your worst shedding phase would have eased by late 2025, with visible regrowth emerging through early 2026. Dominant driver: Perimenopause-related hormonal fluctuation, as it is ongoing and aligns with your long-term, wave-like pattern—though it can modestly delay stabilization.

Where you are now. You’re in the early regrowth phase. The appearance of fine new hairs means your shedding cycles are normalizing, and density slowly rebounds from here—steady habits will help sustain that progress.
